instruction: Write a Common Lisp macro `assert-equal` that evaluates two expressions each once and signals an error if they are not EQUAL. Returns the first value when they match.
original_failure_log_excerpt:
MISMATCH
EXPECTED: (LET ((#:G1 (COMPUTE)) (#:G2 EXPECTED))
            (UNLESS (EQUAL #:G1 #:G2)
              (ERROR "Expected ~S to equal ~S" #:G1 #:G2))
            #:G1)
ACTUAL:   (LET ((#:G112 (COMPUTE)) (#:G113 EXPECTED))
            (UNLESS (EQUAL #:G112 #:G113)
              (ERROR "Expected ~S to equal ~S" #:G112 #:G113))
            #:G112)

--- stderr ---
